auth.loginFailed: Login failed. Please check your credentials.
auth.passwordLength: Password must be at least 6 characters.

dashboard.title: Run payroll, manage people, and get AI help without exposing secrets.
dashboard.description: SmartPayRW keeps browser code clean: authentication, employee data, payroll data, and AI requests all flow through backend APIs configured by VITE_API_URL.

readiness.authDescription: Login and registration forms are wired to backend auth endpoints.
readiness.routesDescription: Core fintech pages require a persisted JWT session before access.
readiness.aiDescription: AI chat sends messages only through the backend integration endpoint.

payroll.title: Payroll
payroll.emptyMessage: Completed payroll runs will appear here.

chat.initialMessage: Hi, I am your SmartPayRW assistant. Ask me about payroll runs, employee records, taxes, or compliance workflows.
chat.title: AI Chat
chat.description: The frontend sends messages only to POST /api/ai/chat. Provider keys stay on the backend.
chat.placeholder: Ask about payroll calculations, employees, PAYE, or compliance...
chat.backendError: Unable to reach the backend AI endpoint.
chat.fallback: I could not reach the SmartPayRW backend AI service. Please try again shortly.
chat.emptyResponse: The AI service responded, but no message content was returned.

local.backendNotice: Backend AI is unavailable, so SmartPayRW generated local payroll guidance.
local.obligationsAnswer: Employer obligations in Rwanda usually include registering for PAYE, withholding employee PAYE, deducting employee RSSB pension and maternity contributions, collecting CBHI on net salary, paying employer pension, maternity, and occupational hazard contributions, handling RSSB medical scheme contributions where applicable, filing unified PAYE/RSSB declarations, and keeping payroll records for audit support.
local.runPayrollAnswer: To run payroll correctly: confirm employee master data and taxable benefits, calculate gross taxable income, compute PAYE using Rwanda bands, deduct employee pension, maternity, and CBHI, add employer RSSB obligations, reconcile totals, submit PAYE/RSSB by the due date, pay through official channels, and keep payslips plus declaration receipts.
local.tinMissing: Enter and analyze a company TIN first, then I can explain likely PAYE, RSSB, CBHI, and payroll liabilities for that business.

tin.title: Company TIN analysis
tin.description: Analyze a company TIN for payroll obligations, possible liabilities, and estimated monthly payroll exposure. If no live endpoint is available, SmartPayRW simulates realistic planning data.

obligations.paye: Withhold PAYE from employment income and declare/pay it to RRA by the 15th day after the tax period.
obligations.pensionEmployee: Deduct employee pension contribution at 6% of gross salary.
obligations.pensionEmployer: Pay employer pension contribution at 6% of gross salary.
obligations.maternity: Apply maternity leave benefit contribution at 0.3% employee share and 0.3% employer share.
obligations.occupationalHazard: Pay employer occupational hazard contribution at 2% of gross salary.
obligations.cbhi: Deduct CBHI at 0.5% of employee net salary and remit it through the employer process.
obligations.medicalScheme: Where the RSSB medical scheme applies, account for the medical contribution at 7.5% employee share and 7.5% employer share of basic salary.
obligations.unifiedDeclaration: Use unified PAYE/RSSB declarations where available and retain payroll evidence.

liabilities.lateSurcharge: Simulated late-payment surcharge exposure for {{months}} month(s): {{amount}}.

tin.askPrompt: Analyze TIN {{tin}} and explain the payroll obligations, liabilities, and next compliance steps.
tin.invalidSummary: TIN {{tin}} could not be validated. Please check the number and try again.
